raw_title: The Promise of a Better Past: Media, Authoritarian Nostalgia, and the Far Right
raw_abstract: The rising electoral support for far-right parties has sparked vibrant debates about the sociopolitical dynamics driving this phenomenon. This paper investigates a crucial yet underexplored dimension: the interplay between media consumption, authoritarian nostalgia, and far-right voting behaviour. Focusing on Spain and Portugal – two countries with distinct yet comparable histories of authoritarian rule – survey data analyses suggest that readers of ABC in Spain and Correio da Manhã (CdM) in Portugal may develop higher levels of authoritarian nostalgia, which in turn correlates with a higher probability to support VOX and Chega, respectively. A content analysis conducted on these newspapers indicates that this pattern may be driven more by the tone adopted by the tabloid (CdM) and conservative ( ABC ) newspapers when discussing the authoritarian past rather than by higher levels of authoritarian nostalgia compared to other newspapers. This study contributes to political communication research by highlighting a mechanism through which media narratives shape historical perceptions and electoral choices.
title_en: The Promise of a Better Past: Media, Authoritarian Nostalgia, and the Far Right
title_zh: 更美好过去的承诺：媒体、威权怀旧与极右翼
abstract_en: The rising electoral support for far-right parties has sparked vibrant debates about the sociopolitical dynamics driving this phenomenon. This paper investigates a crucial yet underexplored dimension: the interplay between media consumption, authoritarian nostalgia, and far-right voting behaviour. Focusing on Spain and Portugal – two countries with distinct yet comparable histories of authoritarian rule – survey data analyses suggest that readers of ABC in Spain and Correio da Manhã (CdM) in Portugal may develop higher levels of authoritarian nostalgia, which in turn correlates with a higher probability to support VOX and Chega, respectively. A content analysis conducted on these newspapers indicates that this pattern may be driven more by the tone adopted by the tabloid (CdM) and conservative (ABC) newspapers when discussing the authoritarian past rather than by higher levels of authoritarian nostalgia compared to other newspapers. This study contributes to political communication research by highlighting a mechanism through which media narratives shape historical perceptions and electoral choices.
abstract_zh: 极右翼政党选举支持率的上升引发了关于推动这一现象的社会政治动态的激烈辩论。本文研究了一个关键但尚未充分探索的维度：媒体消费、威权怀旧与极右翼投票行为之间的相互作用。聚焦于西班牙和葡萄牙这两个具有不同但可比较的威权统治历史的国家，调查数据分析表明，西班牙《ABC》报和葡萄牙《晨邮报》（CdM）的读者可能产生更高水平的威权怀旧情绪，这反过来分别与支持VOX党和Chega党的更高概率相关。对这些报纸进行的内容分析表明，这种模式可能更多地是由小报（CdM）和保守派（ABC）报纸在讨论威权过去时采用的语气驱动的，而不是因为它们比其他报纸具有更高水平的威权怀旧内容。本研究通过强调媒体叙事塑造历史认知和选举选择的机制，为政治传播研究做出了贡献。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了为什么有些人会投票给极右翼政党。研究人员发现，阅读特定报纸（如西班牙的《ABC》和葡萄牙的《晨邮报》）的人更容易怀念过去的威权统治时期，这种怀旧情绪使他们更可能支持极右翼政党。研究显示，这些报纸在报道历史时使用的语气（而不是内容本身）可能是关键因素。这项研究帮助我们理解媒体如何影响人们对历史的看法，进而影响他们的政治选择。
plain_summary_en: This study explores why some people vote for far-right parties. Researchers found that readers of specific newspapers (like ABC in Spain and Correio da Manhã in Portugal) are more likely to feel nostalgic for past authoritarian periods, and this nostalgia makes them more likely to support far-right parties. The study suggests that the tone these newspapers use when discussing history (rather than the content itself) may be the key factor. This research helps us understand how media influences people's views of history and, in turn, their political choices.
research_direction_zh: 探索媒体叙事如何通过塑造历史记忆和怀旧情绪来影响当代政治态度和选举行为，特别是在后威权社会背景下。
research_direction_en: Examining how media narratives influence contemporary political attitudes and electoral behavior by shaping historical memory and nostalgia, particularly in post-authoritarian societies.
